selected_rule.rule_reading: High-beta core expression should not be entry-vetoed outright. Once late main-markup states have held long enough, a drawdown-opened MA5 break can shift the position into reduced expression while still keeping a partial stake alive.

interpretation: V1.12CO tests whether high-beta core residuals are better handled by partial de-risk rather than by entry veto or blanket suppression.
The best rule improves both compounded evidence return and max drawdown while only touching the late high-drawdown window, so the residual now reads as expression-risk that belongs on a de-risk path.
